A two-time cancer survivor has described how she married her "absolute rock" and soulmate who was also diagnosed twice - after the pair met through their charity work Liv Shaw, from Stockport, Greater Manchester, was given a second cancer diagnosis at 23 having lost part of her leg to the disease in 2011 Now 29, she first met her husband Sam at a Teenage Cancer Trust conference as a 16-year-old and was struck by "how funny he was" and how he "always knows how to lift my spirits" They started dating a few years later and "have never looked back", marrying in Las Vegas last year Liv has Li-Fraumeni syndrome (LFS) - a rare inherited condition that increases the likelihood of people developing certain childhood and adult cancers The operations manager, who lives in Reddish, was diagnosed with osteosarcoma – cancer of the bone - at the age of 14

The Labour Party could benefit most from the return of the Supplementary Votes (SV) system in the Greater Manchester mayoral race, a polling expert has said Voters in the region head to the polls on 30 July and will be able to select a first and second choice of candidate as SV comes into replace the first-past-the-post (FPTP) system used at the last election A number of candidates have already declared for the contest , triggered after Andy Burnham's successful campaign to become the MP of Makerfield, leaving the post he has held since 2017 vacant Prof Jonathan Tonge of the University of Liverpool said Labour could pick up second preference votes from Greens and Lib Dems, while Reform could be less likely to see Restore voters put them as second preference due to "bad blood" between the party leaders The system was used in the first two mayoral elections, but was scrapped by the last Conservative government in favour of FPTP system, before it was reintroduced again by Labour Under SCV, candidates need to win more than 50% of the vote in the first round to be come mayor or the top two candidates go into a run-off where second preference votes from eliminated candidates are counted

True crime series 'Life means life': The infamous Greater Manchester killers told they will die in prison 'Just punishment in your case requires you to be kept in prison for the rest of your life' View 12 Images Whole life orders have been around sinc News Damon Wilkinson Reporter 06:04, 04 Jul 2026 "I do not think that either of these prisoners should ever be released from custody," Margaret Thatcher wrote to her then Home Secretary Leon Brittan in 1985. "Their crime was the most hideous and cruel in modern times." Thatcher was referring to Moors Murderers Ian Brady and Myra Hindley . And five years later her wish came true In 1990 Hindley became the first woman in England and Wales to be given a whole-life term, 24 years after she and Brady were first sentenced to life in prison for the sexual abuse, torture and murder of three children. She joined a relatively small number of criminals serving the Whole life orders have been around since 1983. The Sentencing Council says they are for only the most serious cases of murder There were 74 whole-life prisoners in England and Wales as of September 2025, according to Ministry of Justice statistics. Several other inmates have died while serving a whole-life term

Courts The horrific past of murderer who kidnapped girl, 15, and abused her Former radio presenter Paul Sharpe is awaiting his fate from the courts once again News Stephen Topping Senior reporter 19:43, 04 Jul 2026 View 2 Images Paul Sharpe, 56, from A kidnapper who tied a girl up in his car and abused her previously killed a mum who he struck up a relationship with, the Manchester Evening News can reveal. Paul Sharpe is awaiting his fate after abducting a 15-year-old girl at knifepoint, forcing her into his car and sexually The 56-year-old, from Stockport , has admitted his latest sickening crimes and will be sentenced later this year. But it is not the first the Sharpe has committed an abhorrent crime against a female in Greater Manchester Back in October 2000, Sharpe murdered a Bolton woman who he was in a relationship with. The pair had struck up a romance after she listened to Sharpe on a radio show he hosted at the time on Tower FM, under his alias Paul Shaw Sharpe fatally strangled the 28-year-old mum, a beauty therapy student at Bolton College, after she threatened to break up with him. He went on the run, but three days later handed himself in, telling officers he had 'flipped' after the relationship appeared to be reaching an end Sharpe, then of Wellington Road, Bury, was handed a life sentence at Manchester Crown Court the following March. The judge, Mr Justice Penry-Davey, told him: "You deprived her of her life and her son of his mother."

I help by listening to patients and trying to relive old memories and thoughts and give them hope for the future. I try to get them involved in the act

Extensive CCTV enquiries identified Sharpe’s vehicle, and he was arrested in a hotel car park in Knutsford at around 3.40am the following morning with the girl still in the car

A man has described the "amazing" moment Pele and his Brazil teammates visited his hometown in Cheshire The South American side stayed at a hotel in Lymm during their 1966 World Cup campaign, which saw them dumped out of the competition at the group stage But as part of their time in the rural village, the squad, which also included Garrincha and Jairzinho, met with locals and signed autographs Sixty years on, Laurence Bennett, who was just five years old at the time, said he would spend mornings before school serving toast to the players as his mum Bessie worked at the Lymm Hotel "I got to just wander around these magnificent footballers," Bennett told BBC Radio Manchester He said the access people in the village had to the players would never be allowed today

More than 2,000 people will parade through Manchester later in a tradition begun by the city's Italian community nearly 140 years ago The Madonna Del Rosario procession is part of a display of religious faith but also a celebration of Italian identity with pride of place given to a 5ft (1.5m) statue of the Virgin Mary dating back nearly 100 years It begins at 13:00 BST in George Leigh Street, Ancoats, an area once known as Little Italy due to the Italian immigrants who settled there The procession, which began in 1888, leaves Ancoats with association officials and civic dignitaries at its head, led by the Lord Mayor of Manchester and the Bishop of Salford The procession involves the carrying of religious emblems – The Madonna, The Calvary, St Anthony and banners – from Ancoats across Manchester city centre, passing the Town Hall into St Peter's Square around 14:30 After a pause it returns back to George Leigh Street to finish with a short blessing outside the former St Michael's Church

Northern Quarter 'We don't need to turn the lights on at night, we just open up the curtains' The 'overbearing' digital display is to be taken down following a court battle News Damon Wilkinson Reporter 07:19, 05 Jul 2026 View Image The advertising ho A huge digital advertising hoarding in the Northern Quarter which could cause a 'severe' distraction for drivers is to be taken down following a court battle The 'overbearing' and 'intrusive' 19mx5m display on the busy crossroads of Rochdale Road and Addington Street was said to be 'considerably at odds' with the surrounding architecture. The massive billboard, which displays a changing roll of adverts, will now have to removed by Aug It came after Manchester council issued a 'discontinuation notice' on the land ordering the hoarding to be taken down. The council said the 'dominating' hoarding was 'contrary to the overall aims of the regeneration strategies for New Cross, NOMA and the Northern gateway' Because the illuminated sign required 'increased cognitive demand' it also had the potential to cause a 'severe' distraction for drivers and be 'detrimental' to safety, a council inspector said. The Appeal Court heard planning permission for the hoarding was first granted in Sept In 2021 it was replaced by the current digital billboard. A council inspector who visited the site in 2024 described the 'very large' billboard as 'intrusive' and 'overbearing'

Wigan The Latic fanatic, the killer driver and the family left to pick up the pieces After repeatedly running Darryl Tomlinson over, Megan Murphy stepped out of the car, lied to police then called a pal to say she had run over a 'smackhead'. In an int It was 7am when Darryl Tomlinson’s family got the phone call. Mum, Michelle, and Dad, Paul ‘Tommy’ Tomlinson, were celebrating her 50th birthday in Tenerife What followed was unimaginable horror, shock and a long, long journey home Over 12 hours later they arrived back in Wigan to learn that their beloved son had been hit by a car and lost his life. Initially, Paul's instinct was to forgive his son's killer Then he learned the truth of her callous actions In the moments that followed the incident, Megan Murphy lied to the police about what she had done. And as Darryl lay dying, trapped under her car, she called a pal and branded her victim a 'smackhead'

Employment Two very different corners of Greater Manchester, one very similar problem SPECIAL INVESTIGATION Youth unemployment is rising across the country, and The Manchester Evening News has delved into the latest figures View 7 Images A tale of two News Stephen Topping Senior reporter and Richard Ault 07:11, 05 Jul 2026 The numbers are stark. More than one million under-25s across the country are not in education, employment or training according to the latest official figures Greater Manchester is no different. Unemployment is rising among young adults and it's a problem of which political leaders are only too aware Both Sir Keir Starmer and his likely-successor Andy Burnham have been discussing ways to tackle it over recent days. But the problem isn't straightforward The causes of rising youth unemployment appear to be varied - and the end result is being seen in communities with very different demographics The Manchester Evening News has delved into the latest figures from the House of Commons Library which show the number of people aged 18 to 24 who are claiming either Jobseeker’s Allowance or Universal Credit with a requirement to seek work. The data is broken down into parliamen

World Cup Police issue message to fans as pubs to stay open for England v Mexico World Cup game England are playing Mexico at 1am on Monday, July 6, in the World Cup last 16 News Amy Walker Court reporter 17:24, 04 Jul 2026 View 2 Images England's nex Police have issued a warning to fans as pubs are set to remain open during the England v Mexico match at 1am on Monday After suggestions that it would not be possible, the Government announced on Thursday evening (July 2) that pubs would indeed be able to stay open until 5am to ensure fans can watch the crucial World Cup game Due to the match taking place at the Azteca Stadium in Mexico City, the match has a later start time than other matches have done and it meant that it's kick-off did not fall into the originally allowed rules of when pubs could extend their opening hours until And despite rumours FIFA might bring the kick off forward to 7pm on Sunday night, it was later confirmed kick off would remain at 1am on Monday morning Now, as over 80 venues are set to open their doors, Greater Manchester Police have issued a message to fans.
